lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Ah, a stranger to our island. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Ah! [player name], I'm glad to see you've returned. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a very interesting house you have here. Does he have a name?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Why of course. It's Berty.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Berty? Berty the Chicken leg house?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: May I ask why?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: It just has that certain ring to it, don't you think? Beeerteeee!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're ins...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Insane? Very.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: There's a lunar spell in my spell book that I can't use. Could you...?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Help you? Certainly. It's one I've just rediscovered! I can teach it to you if you like, but you have to listen to a story first.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I don't mind hearing sto-

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Wonderful!

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Well, I was going through some ancient documents recently, expanding my knowledge of our great ancestors...

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Hmm, actually, I think I had better go further back, so you will understand.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: You know of our ancient war with the Fremennik that you recently healed?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course! I am a great diplomat, aren't I?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Some diplomat. Did you even discover WHY we were at war for so long?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Errr... You couldn't see eye to eye about magic, or something like that.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Not too far off; it goes deeper than that.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: The Fremennik were actually the first to discover how to make runes, but there was disagreement within the tribe about the implications of making such powerful objects.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Most Fremennik felt we were stealing power from the gods. They could not see past that to the great things we could do with such power, so we split from them and became the Moon Clan. Our ancient leader, who first

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: discovered the rune essence mine here on Lunar Isle, travelled far and wide searching for more places of power. He and his followers built altars all over Gielinor - which you use today to create runes - and at their

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: height they made runes by the crateful. They had perfected the process and even advanced it, creating altars that could create any type of rune regardless of the crafter's ability. But the strife between the Moon

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Clan and the Fremennik heightened. The Fremennik had grown strong in body, while our clan had grown stong in mind. They overpowered our tribe and we retreated to Lunar Isle, where we had lived ever since.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: The Fremennik then swept over Gielinor destroying the altars we had made, in an attempt to remove all trace of magic from the land.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all very interesting, but what's it got to do with this spell?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: I'm getting to that!

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: The Fremennik were not as thorough as they thought. They destroyed only the entrances to the altars, for the altars themselves were on a different plane, but they were successful in wiping out our ability to create

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: runes. That ability has only recently been rediscovered on the mainland, so I hear.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Our people were left isolated on Lunar Isle but we used it to our advantage. Our control over Magic advanced beyond the need for runes and all knowledge of the altar locations passed from memory. Fortunately,

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: some records survived... The manuscript I discovered contains the teleportation spell to an advanced altar called Ourania. It's an altar that allows the user, regardless of their Runecrafting ability, to craft any

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: type of rune upon it. Ourania is a powerful altar indeed. What state it is in after so many millennia, I know not, but it's interesting all the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, will you teach me the spell?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: Certainly.

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: *Baby Yaga whispers in your ear*

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: And now you know the teleportation spell, off with you! Go explore.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't really have ti-

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: *Sigh* Very well. Another time, perhaps.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why do you ask questions if you're going to read my mind anyway?

lines.speaker: Baba Yaga
lines.text: I was being polite, silly foreigner.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm good thanks, bye.
